references.Image 1: Primary room scene, camera, crop, and lighting reference
references.Image 2: Exact product geometry, label, and color reference

preserve: Image 1 camera angle, room geometry, furniture, window, floor shadows, crop, and overall color balance
Image 2 product silhouette, proportions, label spelling, cap, and packaging geometry
changes: place the product from Image 2 on the front-right corner of the table in Image 1
apply only the matte ceramic surface character from Image 3 while keeping the product label crisp and unchanged
composition: The product should occupy about 12 percent of the frame and sit naturally on the table without blocking existing objects
lighting: Match Image 1 light direction, contact shadow, reflections, and color temperature
constraints: do not redesign the product, add copy, move furniture, alter the room, or introduce extra objects
